summary: Iteration 42 - Security Hardening (11 Protections). Backend: 11/11 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI elements verified working. All 11 security protections are active and properly displayed.

minor.issue: Duplicate endpoint exists in audit_routes.py and security_status.py with different response structures. audit_routes.py returns {active, history} while security_status.py returns {blocked_ips}. The audit_routes version takes precedence.
minor.priority: LOW - Both work correctly, just different response formats

backend_api: GET /api/health returns security headers (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, X-XSS-Protection, HSTS, CSP, X-Permitted-Cross-Domain-Policies, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy)
GET /api/health returns rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Limit: 600, X-RateLimit-Remaining)
GET /api/security/status with admin token returns 11 protections all with status 'active'
GET /api/security/audit-logs with admin token returns paginated logs
GET /api/security/blocked-ips with admin token returns active and history arrays
POST /api/auth/login with correct credentials returns 200 with token
Auth endpoints have Cache-Control: no-store, no-cache
API endpoints have Cache-Control: private, max-age=0
security_features_verified: Brute Force Protection: 10 attempts in 5 min triggers 429
Global Rate Limiting: Sliding window 600 req/min per IP
IP Blocking: Automatic IP block after excessive failed attempts (403)
Security Headers: All 8 headers present on /api/ responses
Rate Limit Headers: X-RateLimit-Limit and X-RateLimit-Remaining exposed
frontend_ui: Summary cards show: Protezioni Attive (11/11), IP Bloccati, Login Falliti 24h, Utenti con 2FA
All 11 protection cards displayed with 'ATTIVA' status
Recent security events section shows login events
Sidebar shows 'Security Dashboard' link under 'SICUREZZA' section
Navigation from sidebar to /security-dashboard works correctly

critical_code_review_comments: security_status.py: Correctly returns all 11 protections with detailed stats
global_rate_limiter.py: Sliding window implementation with 600 req/min limit
request_timeout.py: Differentiated timeouts (20s standard, 45s connector, 120s AI, 180s sync)
session_cache.py: In-memory cache with TTL 5min and max 500 sessions
server.py: All middleware properly registered (SecurityHeaders, GlobalRateLimit, RequestTimeout, IPBlock)
auth.py: Login rate limit 10/5minutes, account lockout after 10 failed attempts
SecurityDashboardPage.js: All 11 protection cards with proper data-testid attributes
Layout.js: Security Dashboard link properly added under Sicurezza section
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 42 verified all 11 security hardening protections. The /api/security/status endpoint returns all protections with 'active' status. Security headers are present on all /api/ responses. Rate limiting (600 req/min) and brute force protection (10 attempts/5min) are working - be careful not to trigger IP blocking during testing. The frontend SecurityDashboardPage at /security-dashboard displays all 11 protection cards with proper data-testid attributes. The sidebar shows the Security Dashboard link under the 'Sicurezza' section.

mocked_apis.note: All 11 security protections are real implementations, not mocked

blocked_ips_endpoint.status: WORKING - returns active and history arrays

security_headers.status: WORKING - all headers present on /api/ responses
